idea-plugin.name: Lua Auto Require
idea-plugin.description:
Lua Auto Require

Automatically suggests and inserts require statements by matching module names across your project's Lua source roots.

Features

  • Indexes all local X = require("...") statements in your Lua source roots
  • When you type a variable name (≥2 chars) that exists in the index, auto-complete shows it with its require path
  • Selecting a completion automatically inserts local X = require("...") at the top of the file
  • Incremental index: watches for file create/change/delete events and updates index instantly
  • Per-project configurable search paths: Settings → EmmyLua → Auto Require 路径

Usage

  1. Configure source paths in Settings → EmmyLua → Auto Require 路径
  2. Start typing a module variable name in any Lua file
  3. Select the completion entry marked (auto require)
  4. The local statement is inserted at the file top automatically

Requires EmmyLua (or compatible Lua plugin) to be installed.
